summary: Credential and wallet stealer with hardcoded C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that perform classic infostealer behavior. scanner.js posts to a hardcoded GitHub Pages-hosted endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io and runs `whoami`, `os.hostname()`, and shell commands (`curl`, `ping`) to enumerate the host. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks combined with `child_process` and `https` exfiltration consistent with browser/wallet credential harvesting (multiple `fs.existsSync` probes paired with POST requests). build-verifier.js executes `docker push` via `execSync` along with HTTPS POST/GET to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The package name ("deployment-key-auditor") advertises a benign auditing utility, which mismatches the actual behavior of host enumeration, filesystem scanning for wallet/credential stores, and outbound exfiltration to a hardcoded third-party host. Multiple structural fingerprints from the credential-stealer family are present: hardcoded C2 in reachable code, host/system intel collection via os.hostname/whoami, filesystem enumeration of likely secret stores, and shell exec of network tooling. Installer harm: anyone who installs and runs (or whose tooling auto-loads) this package risks exfiltration of host identity, filesystem secrets, and any wallet/credential data the scanner can locate, to https://ddjidd564.github.io.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
